# Doorman

> Doorman (`libdoorman`) is a macOS-only C library that ports the Linux login
> model to macOS: PAM-style authentication transactions with a conversation
> callback, user/group/session enumeration, privilege-dropping session launch,
> and Linux-shaped account provisioning (useradd/passwd/groupadd analogues) —
> all backed by native macOS mechanisms (OpenDirectory, dsLocal ShadowHashData,
> OpenPAM, dscl/dseditgroup/createhomedir).

Key facts an agent should know before generating code against Doorman:

- License: MIT — freely embeddable in open-source or proprietary software.
- Platform: macOS only. Language: C ABI (usable from C, Objective-C, C++,
  Swift via bridging header, or any FFI). Single header: `doorman/include/doorman.h`.
- Link line: `-framework Foundation -framework OpenDirectory -framework Security -lpam -lobjc`
  plus `libdoorman.a` or `libdoorman.dylib`.
- Every function returns `doorman_result_t`; `DOORMAN_SUCCESS == 0`.
- Provisioning (create/delete users/groups, set passwords) requires root and
  returns `DOORMAN_ERR_PERM` otherwise. Verification and enumeration do not.
- The complete machine-friendly context (full signatures, contracts, recipes)
  is in `llms-full.txt` at the repository root.

## Docs

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): the single self-contained context file for
  LLMs/agents — full API signatures, semantic contracts, memory-ownership
  rules, error codes, and copy-paste recipes for every capability.
- [docs/API.md](docs/API.md): human-oriented complete API reference with
  installation, linking, and worked examples per capability.
- [doorman/include/doorman.h](doorman/include/doorman.h): the authoritative
  public header (heavily commented; the source of truth for signatures).
- [README.md](README.md): project overview, install (prebuilt releases, Nix,
  make), and quick-start usage.
- [docs/CLI_AND_PROVISIONING.md](docs/CLI_AND_PROVISIONING.md): the `doorman`
  CLI and the Linux tool shims (useradd/userdel/passwd/groupadd/groupdel/
  usermod/gpasswd).
- [docs/SECURITY.md](docs/SECURITY.md): threat model, secret-scrubbing,
  name-validation, and privilege-handling guarantees.

## Optional

- [docs/AUTH_DIFFERENCES.md](docs/AUTH_DIFFERENCES.md): exhaustive
  macOS-vs-Linux authentication difference map with per-area bridge notes.
- [docs/LINUX_AUTH.md](docs/LINUX_AUTH.md): background on how Linux
  authenticates users and how Doorman ports that model.
- [examples/macdm/macdm.c](examples/macdm/macdm.c): minimal console display
  manager consuming the library end to end.
- [tests/test_doorman.m](tests/test_doorman.m): unit suite touching every
  public entry point (useful as behavioural examples).
